name: Marcus
communication_level: minimally verbal with 5-10 consistent spoken words (mama, no, more, go, up, done, eat, Bluey); uses PECS book for 15+ requests; learning 8 ASL signs; emerging use of AAC device (TouchChat) with 24-icon grid
interests: Bluey (the cartoon  -  watches specific episodes on repeat, acts out scenes)
bubbles (will attend to any activity if bubbles are involved)
stacking and lining up blocks (builds towers to exact heights)
musical instruments (drums especially  -  loves rhythm patterns)

seeks: oral input (chews on chew tubes, clothing collars, crunchy foods)
deep pressure (being squeezed, crawling through tunnels)
avoids: bright overhead fluorescent lights (covers eyes, drops to floor)
calming_strategies: listening to Bluey theme song on headphones

reinforcers: bubbles (blown by adult or bubble machine)
drumming on the classroom bongo drum

iep_goals.description: Marcus will independently use his AAC device (TouchChat) or PECS to make a request (for an item, activity, or help) in 5 out of 10 opportunities across school settings (classroom, cafeteria, playground), as measured by SLP and teacher communication log.
iep_goals.measurement_method: SLP and teacher communication log  -  independent requests via AAC/PECS

trial_history.notes: Uses PECS for 'bubbles' and 'more' consistently. AAC device still novel  -  presses random icons. Needs full physical prompt for AAC requests.
Found the Bluey icon on AAC and pressed it independently 3 times. PECS requests expanding  -  added 'drum' and 'eat' cards. Still needs gestural prompt for most AAC use.
Used AAC to request 'bubbles' in the cafeteria with only a point prompt to the device. SLP modeling AAC use during Bluey watching  -  Marcus imitating 'play' and 'more' buttons.
Independently requested 'drum' on AAC during music time  -  no prompt! PECS still preferred for cafeteria. Combining modalities is fine  -  we want functional communication in any form.
Using AAC for 'help' when stuck on block tower  -  huge functional gain. Said 'more' verbally while pressing AAC button simultaneously. Multimodal communication emerging.
Consistent gains. Navigated to correct AAC page for 'playground' request independently. PECS used 6 times today across settings. Goal is achievable by review date at this pace.

iep_goals.description: Marcus will independently complete the school toileting routine (enter bathroom, pull down pants, use toilet, wipe, pull up pants, flush, wash hands) with no more than 1 verbal prompt for 4 out of 5 scheduled bathroom trips, as measured by toileting data sheet.
iep_goals.measurement_method: toileting data sheet  -  steps completed independently per trip

trial_history.notes: Completes 4/7 steps independently. Needs physical prompt for wiping and verbal prompt for flushing. Visual schedule posted in bathroom stall. Resists hand washing (wet texture).
Flushing now independent after we added a Bluey sticker on the flush handle. Hand washing improving with warm water only (no cold). Still needs wiping support.
Added Bluey visual sequence cards. Marcus checks each card and does the step. Wiping attempt is there but incomplete  -  OT working on motor planning. 5/7 steps independent.
Big progress  -  completed full routine with only 1 verbal prompt (for wiping) on 3 out of 5 trips. Sings Bluey song during hand washing. Tolerating the routine well.
4 out of 5 trips with ≤1 prompt. One accident during afternoon (didn't signal in time  -  was engaged in block play). Adding a visual timer reminder for scheduled trips.
Wiping improving with the adapted wipe holder OT recommended. Full routine independent on 3 trips, 1 prompt needed on other 2. Mom reports similar progress at home.

iep_goals.description: Marcus will safely navigate playground equipment (climb ladder, go down slide, cross monkey bars with support) and follow playground safety rules (wait turn, sit on slide, feet first) in 4 out of 10 recess opportunities, as measured by adapted PE teacher and aide observation log.
iep_goals.measurement_method: adapted PE teacher and aide observation log during recess

trial_history.notes: Avoids climbing equipment  -  sits at base of slide watching other kids. Will go down slide only if aide carries him up. Crowd avoidance limits playground access.
Introduced playground during quiet time (fewer kids). Climbed 3 ladder rungs with aide next to him. Went down small slide independently. Smiled after  -  first positive playground moment.
Climbed full ladder to small slide platform. Went down feet first (safety rule followed!). Still won't attempt during crowded recess  -  only early access with 2-3 peers.
Waited for turn behind one peer (first time!). Aide used Bluey visual card showing 'Bluey waits for her turn.' Motor planning on climbing improving  -  OT adding obstacle course practice.
Attempted big slide with aide spotting  -  went halfway up, got scared, came back down safely (which counts as safe navigation!). Used 'help' on AAC when stuck.
Completed big slide for the first time! Whole class cheered and he laughed. Followed 'feet first' rule. Tolerated 5 peers on equipment at same time. Aide stepping back gradually.
